Select relevant rows.
The question aims to find out which engine Team Penske used in the years after 2011. We need to look at the rows where the year is greater than 2011 to see what engine was used.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
the engine -> engine
team penske -> team
column value of the question link to columns:
2011 -> year
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
after 2011 -> year
team penske used -> team, engine

The question is asking to determine the type of engine used by Team Penske in the years after 2011. Since each row contains information about a specific year, engine, and team, we need to filter the data by the "team" column to isolate Team Penske's entries, and then examine the corresponding "engine" values for the years after 2011. We can use the f_filter(team == "team penske" & year > 2011) operation to narrow down the relevant data points.

The question is asking to determine which engine Team Penske used after the year 2011. To find out, we need to filter the data for years greater than 2011 and then look at the "engine" column for Team Penske. Since the data is already in chronological order, we can simply check the "engine" value for Team Penske in the years 2012 and 2013 where the year is greater than 2011.
After 2011, Team Penske used the Chevrolet engine in both 2012 and 2013. Therefore, the engine used by Team Penske after 2011 was Chevrolet.
